text: Seidr is premature: building abstraction before second consumer exists means choosing shape blind
Portable skill definitions are a category error: execution strategy is scaffolding-specific, only intent/constraints/capabilities are portable
MCP-only interface contradicts stated model-agnostic goals — should be HTTP REST with MCP adapter
Pi resource analysis used wrong spec (4GB vs documented 8GB), overall analysis was sloppy
Munin can serve conventions as documents for now — Seidr's delta is mostly template expansion (~50 lines of logic)
Multi-agent coordination belongs in execution layer (Hugin), not context layer (Seidr) — draft conflated the two
SCION/Hugin improvements are the grounded near-term priority based on observed pain, not hypothetical portability
'Build the second thing first, abstract later' is wiser — turns portability from imagined to observed requirement
Setting up ollama is not the same as building the second consumer — model routing != scaffolding independence
First experiment task must exercise real portability boundary (conventions, capability discovery, tool discipline), not just summarization
Revised plan lacks explicit decision gates — what findings would justify Munin-only vs shared library vs Seidr?
Skills audit should be grounded by implementation experience, not done as desk taxonomy before the consumer exists
